evidence_quotes.quote: A game The Crew by Ubisoft, was recently rendered completely inaccessible after the company decided to shut down its servers.
Many publishers are now able to revoke access to content we have legally bought, undermining consumer rights and eroding trust.

paragraphs.text: I am writing to express my strong support for the Digital Fairness Act and the growing Stop Killing Games initiative, which now has over 1.4 million supporters worldwide. This issue is not theoreticalit has personally affected me. A game The Crew by Ubisoft, was recently rendered completely inaccessible after the company decided to shut down its servers. Despite having paid for the game, I no longer have any way to play it. This experience has left me deeply frustrated and concerned about the direction of digital ownership in the gaming industry. Many publishers are now able to revoke access to content we have legally bought, undermining consumer rights and eroding trust. It is crucial that fair digital ownership principles are established and enforced, so that consumers are not at the mercy of unilateral decisions by corporations. The Digital Fairness Act has the potential to restore balance and accountability in the digital market. I sincerely hope it will move forward with strong provisions that protect consumers from losing access to what they rightfully own.
